posts.name: Matej Roguljic
posts.text:
Dear Combine experts,
For the purpose of generating toys does the  --toysFrequentist option

randomly evaluate nuisance parameters using the postfit central values and uncertainties?

or

set the nuisance parameters to their postfit central values

I tried reading the section on toy generation options, but it was not clear to me.
Thanks,
Matej
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Combine experts,

For the purpose of generating toys does the --toysFrequentist option

  1. randomly evaluate nuisance parameters using the postfit central values and uncertainties?

or

  1. set the nuisance parameters to their postfit central values

I tried reading the section on toy generation options, but it was not clear to me.

Thanks,
Matej

posts.name: Aliya Nigamova
posts.text:
Dear Matej,
With  the --toysFrequentist option combine randomises the NPs around the post-fit values within the post-fit uncertainties, so the case 1. as you described it.
Best,
Aliya
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Matej,

With the --toysFrequentist option combine randomises the NPs around the post-fit values within the post-fit uncertainties, so the case 1. as you described it.

Best,
Aliya

posts.name: Adinda de Wit
posts.text:
Dear Matej, Aliya,
One specification: it’s true that when running with --toysFrequentist the nuisance parameters are not ignored, but it’s not the nuisance parameters themselves that are randomized. They are set to the post-fit values, and the ‘global observables’ (= the gaussian constraints) are randomized. While we generate starting from the post-fit nuisance parameter values, the width of the gaussian is unchanged (ie the pre-fit constraints are used, not the post-fit)
Best,
Adinda
posts.cooked_html:
Dear Matej, Aliya,

One specification: it’s true that when running with --toysFrequentist the nuisance parameters are not ignored, but it’s not the nuisance parameters themselves that are randomized. They are set to the post-fit values, and the ‘global observables’ (= the gaussian constraints) are randomized. While we generate starting from the post-fit nuisance parameter values, the width of the gaussian is unchanged (ie the pre-fit constraints are used, not the post-fit)

Best,
Adinda

posts.name: Aliya Nigamova
posts.text:
Ah, yes, sorry for confusion and thanks for important clarifications.
Best,
Aliya
posts.cooked_html:
Ah, yes, sorry for confusion and thanks for important clarifications.

Best,
Aliya
